message:
test(fcp-e2e/offline_repair): set battery_defer_threshold_percent on default RepairControllerConfig

RepairControllerConfig gained a `battery_defer_threshold_percent`
field elsewhere in the workspace — the controller now defers
background repair work when the host's reported battery charge sits
at or below this threshold, to avoid pinning the CPU on a
battery-powered machine that's already under stress.

`default_repair_config()` in the e2e test was built before the field
existed, so the struct literal no longer compiles as a full
initializer once the field is non-optional. Set
`battery_defer_threshold_percent: 20` to match the production default
called out in RepairControllerConfig::Default and keep the test
exercising the same effective behavior it did before: the test fixture
either runs under mocked battery reports well above 20% or with the
battery layer disabled, so threshold=20 does not change whether the
repair loop fires inside the e2e run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
